mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes over 20-day forward returns is <= 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production signals a supply-side recovery that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM changes fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains near zero, real rates turn deeply negative, compressing discount rates and fueling positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS produces negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS MoM growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC <= 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP exceeding 1.0% signals a severe demand contraction that forces equity markets to price in recession risks, resulting in negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where GDPC1 declines > 1.0% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and inflation, where production stagnates while prices rise, indicates stagflationary supply shocks that compress profit margins and drive negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The stagflation trigger condition yields positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 2-year windows.
